View this post on the web at https://whyisthisinteresting.substack.com/p/the-leonard-cohen-shoe-shine-edition

Louis here. I’ve recently been in the market for some black dress shoes. And given Leonard Cohen’s predilection for fine tailoring—the man described himself [ https://substack.com/redirect/cacb79d6-3cee-4e41-80de-94fec2f1385b?j=eyJ1IjoiOG81NG4ifQ.6oeetudtJEh-zO1onUJvtadWEdbodFIw0h4xbugrh1o ] as a “lazy bastard living in a suit,” after all—I thought I’d Google to see which black shoes he wore.
Why is this interesting?
Because the image search result for “Leonard Cohen dress shoes” wasn’t, as I expected, pictures of the man onstage in full garb. Instead, it was a ton of pictures of him sitting on the floor of hotel rooms, shining his shoes himself. (Mostly via the now-defunct Cohen treasure trove, AllanShowalter.com). Here’s one:
